'''Hull Kingston Rovers 2015 season''' is their 134th season in existence, and their 9th competing in the [[Super League]]. The Robins made a host of changes to the playing staff for the 2015 season, bringing in 13 new players,<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/a-guide-to-our-new-signings|title=A guide to our new signings…|date=17 December 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=31 December 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141213064004/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/a-guide-to-our-new-signings|archive-date=13 December 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> and releasing 18.

The 2015 season is Head Coach [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]]'s first full season in charge, after replacing [[Craig Sandercock]] midway through last season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/club/news/article/club-statement10|title=Club Statement.|date=3 July 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140711074959/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/club/news/article/club-statement10|archive-date=11 July 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> It sees the addition of two new assistant coaches to support the rookie Head Coach, firstly, experienced Assistant [[Willie Poaching]] from the [[Warrington Wolves]],<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/robins-poach-warrington-assistant|title=Robins poach Warrington assistant.|date=5 September 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150103224222/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/robins-poach-warrington-assistant|archive-date=3 January 2015|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> and secondly, rookie Assistant Coach [[David Hodgson (rugby league)|David Hodgson]], who takes up the duties after retiring from playing at the end of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/hodgson-confirmed-as-assistant-coach|title=Hodgson confirmed as assistant coach.|date=3 October 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=3 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150103215707/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/hodgson-confirmed-as-assistant-coach|archive-date=3 January 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> These new Assistants replace the outgoing [[Stanley Gene]], who moved to permanently take up the position of Head Coach at the [[Newcastle Thunder]]<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/gene-leaves-the-robins|title=Gene leaves the Robins.|date=1 October 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=3 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150103215423/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/gene-leaves-the-robins|archive-date=3 January 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> at the end of the 2014 season, and the position previously occupied by Chester himself.

2015 also sees the promotion of halfback and U-19's player of the year Matty Marsh to the first team squad.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/marsh-rewarded-with-first-team-deal|title=Marsh rewarded with first team deal.|date=6 September 2014|work=Hull KR|publisher=hullkr.co.uk|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150103224358/http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/marsh-rewarded-with-first-team-deal|archive-date=3 January 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>
